snippets.title: One-hot encoding with pandas get_dummies
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"color": ["red", "blue", "red", "green"]})
encoded = pd.get_dummies(df, columns=["color"], dtype=int)
print(encoded)
snippets.explanation: Each distinct color becomes its own binary column. Fit vocabulary on training data only in production pipelines.

snippets.title: Ordinal encoding with explicit order
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd

order = {"low": 0, "medium": 1, "high": 2}
df = pd.DataFrame({"risk": ["low", "high", "medium"]})
df["risk_ord"] = df["risk"].map(order)
print(df)
snippets.explanation: The mapping encodes true ranking. Unknown labels should map to NaN or a dedicated unknown code—not arbitrary integers.

snippets.title: sklearn OneHotEncoder with handle_unknown
snippets.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
import numpy as np

X_train = np.array([["a"], ["b"], ["a"]])
X_test = np.array([["b"], ["c"]])  # "c" unseen at train
enc = OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False)
enc.fit(X_train)
print(enc.transform(X_test))
snippets.explanation: `handle_unknown='ignore'` yields all-zero for unseen "c" instead of raising an error—document that behavior for downstream models.

snippets.title: Leakage-safe target encoding sketch (out-of-fold means)
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold

df = pd.DataFrame({"city": ["A", "B", "A", "B", "A"], "price": [100, 200, 110, 190, 105]})
df["city_target"] = float("nan")
kf = KFold(n_splits=3, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
for train_idx, val_idx in kf.split(df):
    means = df.iloc[train_idx].groupby("city")["price"].mean()
    df.loc[val_idx, "city_target"] = df.iloc[val_idx]["city"].map(means)
print(df)
snippets.explanation: Each row's encoded value uses only other rows' targets in the same fold—avoiding direct label leakage from the row itself.
